novel_features.name: Price-research surfacing
novel_features.description: See a listing's MSRP, average listing price, and deal rating as typed fields, not buried prose.
novel_features.rationale: MotoHunt computes MSRP/ALP/deal-rating per listing; we expose them as structured fields an agent can compare.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to answer 'is this listing actually a good price?' without reading the page.

novel_features.name: Offline deal ranking
novel_features.description: Rank synced listings by how far the asking price sits below the average listing price.
novel_features.rationale: Requires syncing many listings into a local store and ranking by ask-vs-ALP gap; the site deal-rates one listing at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to surface the biggest under-market deals across a whole search.

novel_features.name: Saved-search watch + diff
novel_features.description: Re-run saved searches and report new listings and price drops since the last run.
novel_features.rationale: Requires snapshot history in the local store; the site has no alerts for anonymous users.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to monitor a hunt over time instead of re-searching by hand.

novel_features.name: Dual-marketplace parity
novel_features.description: Search motorcycles (motohunt.com) or ATV/UTV/SxS (atvhunt.com) from one binary via --site.
novel_features.rationale: atvhunt is the same engine and HTML; one CLI covers both marketplaces.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for --site atv when the hunt is four-wheelers instead of bikes.

narrative.headline: Search motorcycle and ATV listings from the terminal — with MotoHunt's MSRP/average-price/deal-rating data exposed as structured fields no other tool gives you.
narrative.value_prop: MotoHunt has no public API; this CLI scrapes its server-rendered HTML with one HTTP GET per page and returns clean JSON. It surfaces the price-research data (base MSRP, average listing price, deal rating) that makes 'is this a good deal?' answerable, ranks synced inventory by under-market gap with `deal`, watches saved searches for new listings and price drops, and covers the ATV sister site via `--site atv`.

quickstart.comment: health check + selector-health probe, no network needed
best-deal Harleys near a ZIP, parsed cards as JSON
one listing's price-research at a glance
same search against the ATV marketplace

troubleshoots.symptom: search returns zero cards but the site shows results
troubleshoots.fix: the page HTML changed; run `motohunt-pp-cli doctor` to check selector health and re-verify the card selectors
raise --limit; the CLI auto-pages via ?start= in 24-row pages up to --max-pages

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or script needs to search used motorcycle or ATV inventory, pull a listing's specs and price-research, rank a search by under-market deal gap, or watch a saved search for new listings and price drops. It is the only programmatic access to MotoHunt's MSRP/ALP/deal-rating data.

recipes.title: Best-deal Harleys near me
recipes.explanation: Top under-market Harleys, slimmed to the fields that matter.

recipes.title: Is this listing a good price?
recipes.explanation: Compare the ask against MSRP and the average listing price.

recipes.title: Watch a hunt for price drops
recipes.command: motohunt-pp-cli watch add --name 'gs near me' --make BMW --model R-1250-GS --location 33705 && motohunt-pp-cli watch run --agent
recipes.explanation: Save a search, then diff it over time for new listings and price drops.

trigger_phrases: is this bike a good deal
